data.name: DataBinding_BoundColumnDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to setup one or more bound data fields and bind the grid to the different supported data sources (e.g. Data Source Controls, Data Tables, ICollection, IEnumerable, and IBindingList.

data.name: DataBinding_BoundColumnTitle
data.value: Databinding bound data fields to different data sources

data.name: DataBinding_TemplateColumnDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. This sample shows how to use templates with databinding expressions

data.name: DataBinding_TemplateColumnTitle
data.value: Using DataBinding Expressions and Column Templates

data.name: DataBinding_BasicDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how easy it is to bind a data source control on the page by setting the grid’s DataSourceID property to equal the ID of the data source control.

data.name: DataBinding_DynamicColumnDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to use code to dynamically create the bound columns of the grid and bind a data source.

data.name: DataBinding_DynamicColumnTitle
data.value: Adding columns dynamically to the WebDataGrid

data.name: Paging_BasicDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. The behavior includes properties for setting the number of items displayed per page and position of the pager can be set to top, bottom, or both.

data.name: Paging_CustomDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. This sample shows how to use a custom CSS style to customize the appearance of the grid’s pager.

data.name: Paging_CustomTitle
data.value: How to use CSS styles to customize the pager

data.name: Paging_PreviousNextDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. This sample shows how to setup the pager mode to be NextPrevious. This mode allows you to use first, last, previous, and next links to navigate between pages instead of using a list of pages.

data.name: Paging_PreviousNextTitle
data.value: How to setup a Previous\Next Pager

data.name: Paging_TemplateDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. This sample shows how to create a custom pager appearance using the pager’s template.

data.name: Paging_TemplateTitle
data.value: How to build a custom pager

data.name: TemplatesAlternate_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. This sample shows how to use alternating row template to display every other row using a different CSS style (e.g. changing the text color).

data.name: TemplatesCell_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. This sample shows how to use custom cell/column templates to alter the appearance of a cell or column. When the order quantity is 25 or greater than the quantity cell will be highlighted.

data.name: TemplatesEditRow_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. This sample shows how to use an edit row template to offer a custom editing experience. Double click on a row to open the edit row template. After modifying one or more data elements click OK to update the grid or Cancel to undo your changes.

data.name: TemplatesEmpty_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. This sample shows how to setup a custom error and empty row template. Change the currently displayed template in the control panel to see the configure grid template.

data.name: TemplatesEmpty_Title
data.value: Using Error and Empty Row Templates

data.name: TemplatesHeader_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several templates that allow you to customize how the grid is displayed. In this sample a custom header and footer have been setup. The customer head includes a select all check box that is used for selecting all the currently displayed rows. Each column can use a different header and footer template.

data.name: AppStyling_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid ships with a number of pre-built application styles. This sample allows you to quickly change the appearance of the grid by applying one of the pre-build styles.

data.name: ColumnResizing_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a column resizing behavior that allows you to configure the grid to support resizing of columns. This sample shows how to setup the behavior so an end user can resize one or more columns.

data.name: CustomCSS_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid contains several CSS properties that can be used to control the appearance of the grid. This sample shows how to use custom CSS styles to setup the grid to look like a web based email client.

data.name: Activation_ClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an activation behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when a cell is active. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events of the activation behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: Activation_CustomCSSDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an activation behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when a cell is active. This sample shows how to use a custom CSS style to customize the appearance of the active cell, column, or row.

data.name: Activation_BasicDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an activation behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when a cell is active. This sample shows how to enable or disable the activation behavior. A custom CSS style is used to highlight the active cell when the behavior is enabled. When it is disabled the custom CSS style is not applied.

data.name: Activation_ServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an activation behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when a cell is active. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events of the activation behavior and displays the contents of the active cell when the server side event is fired.

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an add new row behavior that allows an end user to add one or more rows of data to the bound data source. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events of the behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowClientTitle
data.value: Add New Row Behavior - Client Events

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an add new row behavior that allows an end user to add one or more rows of data to the bound data source. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events of the behavior. When the Row Adding event fires, the length of the ID column is checked to make sure it is at least five characters. If the length is smaller the end user is notified using the AJAX response property of the grid.

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowServerTitle
data.value: Add New Row Behavior - Server Events

data.name: DataEditing_AutoCrudDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid can be configured to automatically handle CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) actions against custom collections. This sample shows how to enable the grid to perform the CRUD operations. To add a new row, please hit Enter after updating the last cell of the new row.

data.name: DataEditing_DataFilterClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a data filtering behavior that allows you to enable filtering of data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events of the behavior and notifies the user when the events fire.

data.name: DataEditing_DataFilterDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a data filtering behavior that allows you to enable filtering of data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to configure the alignment, visibility, and animation of the data filtering control. This control is used for filtering data by applying one of the following filtering rules: Equals, begin with, end with, contains, does not contain, or does not equal.

data.name: DataEditing_DataFilterServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a data filtering behavior that allows you to enable filtering of data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events of the behavior and displays the filtering rule and value used for filtering data.

data.name: DataEditing_DeleteRowDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a delete row behavior that allows an end user to remove one or more rows of data from the bound data source. This sample shows how to setup the behavior so when the user clicks on the delete icon the current row is deleted from the bound data source.

data.name: DataEditing_DeleteRowServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a delete row behavior that allows an end user to remove one or more rows of data from the bound data source. This sample shows how to interact with the server events of the behavior. When the Row Deleting event fires, the row trying to be deleted is checked. If the row Id equals “LONEP”, a validation message is returned to user using the grid’s AJAX response property. All other rows will be deleted from the data source.

data.name: DataEditing_EditModeDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid support several different editing modes. This sample shows how to configure the grid to use the different edit mode available: Single or Double Click, F2, Key Press, or on Activation.

data.name: Selection_RowSelectorsDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a row selector behavior that allows you to configure how row selection behaves. This sample shows how to setup the grid to display row selectors and row numbering. The behavior can be configured to show or hide row numbering

data.name: Selection_ClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a selection behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when one or more cells, columns, or rows are selected. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events of the behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: Selection_BasicDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a selection behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when one or more cells, columns, or rows are selected. This sample uses the client side events of the selection behavior to keep track of the number of selected cell and columns.

data.name: Selection_ServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a selection behavior that allows you configure how the grid behaves when one or more cells, columns, or rows are selected. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events of the behavior and display the contents of the selected cell.

data.name: Sorting_ClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a sorting behavior that allows for one or more columns to be used for sorting the data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events for the behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: Sorting_CustomIndicatorsDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a sorting behavior that allows for one or more columns to be used for sorting the data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to use an image as a custom sorting indicator.

data.name: Sorting_BasicDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a sorting behavior that allows for one or more columns to be used for sorting the data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to setup the behavior to support single or multi column sorting. When multi column mode is enabled use the Ctrl key to select one or more columns to sort the data by.

data.name: Sorting_ServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a sorting behavior that allows for one or more columns to be used for sorting the data bound to the grid. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events for the behavior and displays the current sorted column and direction when the event fires.

data.name: DataEditing_UpdateRowClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a cell editing behavior that allows an end user to edit one or more data elements in the bound data source. This sample shows how to interact with the Client-side events for the behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: DataEditing_UpdateRowDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a cell editing behavior that allows an end user to edit one or more data elements in the bound data source. This sample shows how to setup the grid to allow basic cell editing. After changing one or more data elements click on the “Save Changes” button to update the data source.

data.name: DataEditing_UpdateRowServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a cell editing behavior that allows an end user to edit one or more data elements in the bound data source. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events for the behavior. When the Row Updating event fires, it checks to see if row being edit has a validate company name. If the company name is blank, the end user is notified using the AJAX response property of the grid. If the company name is not blank, the data source is updated and the user is notified of the successful operation.

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes an add new row behavior that allows an end user to add one or more rows of data to the bound data source. This sample shows how to setup the behavior so the new row can be displayed at the top or bottom of the grid.

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowClient_HeaderTitle
data.value: Client-side Grid Add New Row Events

data.name: DataEditing_AddRow_DescriptionText
data.value: Enter data into the blank row; once you <TAB> or press <ENTER> to end editing on the last cell the row will be added to the grid.

data.name: WebDataGrid_EventsDescription
data.value: Interact with the grid to see event that's raised

data.name: DataEditing_AddRowServer_HeaderTitle
data.value: Server Side Grid Add New Row Events

data.name: DataEditing_DeleteRowServer_HeaderTitle
data.value: Server Side Grid Delete Row Events

data.name: DataEditing_UpdateRowServer_HeaderTitle
data.value: Server Side Grid Update Row Events

data.name: Paging_Basic_PagerPosition_Item3
data.value: Pager on both top and bottom

data.name: Selection_Basic_Description
data.value: Click and drag to select multiple cells, or hold Control + Click on the Headers to select multiple columns.

data.name: Selection_Basic_HeaderTitle
data.value: Multiple Cell and Header Selection Status:

data.name: Sorting_Basic_EventsHeaderTitle
data.value: Sort a single column of data by clicking a column header. Sort multiple columns by pressing the Ctrl key while clicking multiple column headers.

data.name: Activation_ServerEventMessage
data.value: Activation Event Fired! Active Cell Value:

data.name: Sorting_ServerSideEventMessage
data.value: Sorting Event Fired! Sorting by Column: {0} Sort Direction: {1}

data.name: AddNewRow_ServerMessageFail
data.value: New row failed validation. Customer ID {0} is less than 5 characters

data.name: RowDelete_ServerMessageSuccess
data.value: The following rows have been deleted {0}.

data.name: ErrorTemplateException
data.value: An error has occurred processing your request!

data.name: ErrorTemplateMessage
data.value: An error has occurred! Please contact support.

data.name: Dashboard_Description
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that can be used in several different scenarios. This sample shows how to use the grid in a sales dashboard. Select a row to display sales data by month for the selected customer in a chart.

data.name: Paging_ClientDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. This sample shows how to interact with the client side events of the paging behavior and notifies the user when the events are fired.

data.name: Paging_ServerDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes a paging behavior that allows you configure the grid to display data across multi pages. This sample shows how to interact with the server side events of the paging behavior and notify the user when the events are fired.

data.name: DataEditing_EditorsDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid includes several built in editor providers that can be used to customize the editing experience. This sample shows how to configure the grid to use the following editor providers: Text Editor, Date Chooser, Numeric Editor, and Slider Editor.

data.name: ColumnMoving_Description
data.value: Change one or more of the attributes below to change how column moving behaves. If slide mode is enabled the column header being moved will only follow the x-axis of your mouse. If you select follow mode than the column header will follow both the mouse's x-axis and y-axis. You can disable column moving by selecting one of the columns listed in the disable column list.

data.name: Columns_ColumnMovingDescription
data.value: How to use the column moving behavior of the WebDataGrid.

data.name: EditorDescription
data.value: This samples show how to use editors within the WebDataGrid. For example, double click on the Contact to get a WebDropDown.

data.name: Editors_DropDownDescription
data.value: How to use the WebDropDown as an editor for the WebDataMenu.

data.name: VirtualScrolling_ClientDescription
data.value: This sample demonstrates how to interact with the client side events for the virtual scrolling behavior. This sample will allow you to turn the Virtual Scrolling behavior on and off. You also have the ability to switch between the two available Scrolling Modes.

data.name: VirtualScrolling_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how to configure the different properties of the virtual scrolling behavior. The Row Cache Factor is a multiplier used to determine the total number of fetched rows per request. The Threshold Factor is a multiplier used to define a threshold for requesting a new set of rows from the server. Both the Row Cache Factor and Threshold factor are based on the number of visible rows. The Data Fetch Delay is the number of milliseconds between the last interaction with the scrollbar and the data request.

data.name: VirtualScrolling_PagerDescription
data.value: This sample demonstrates how paging and virtual scrolling work together.

data.name: VirtualScrolling_PagerTitle
data.value: Using a pager with virtual scrolling

data.name: ColumnFixingServer_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how turn on the Column Fixing behavior and some of the adjustments you can make to the behavior. This sample will let you adjust the appearance of the Column Fixing behavior.

data.name: ColumnFixing_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how to configure the different properties of the column fixing behavior. After you modify one of the values the company field will be updated to reflect the change. When synchronize cell height is enabled the cell height for a row will be synchronized. This should only be enabled for small grids because it slows down the column fixing behavior.

data.name: Columns_ColumnFixingClient_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how to programmatically setup column fixing to left and right fix columns. To fix columns using client-side code, you must enable the column fixing feature on the server-side.

data.name: WebDataGridWithContextMenu_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how you can use Context Menu together with WebDataGrid to perform some actions against the selected row.

data.name: WebDataGrid_DataEditing_AutoDetectEditorsDescription
data.value: This sample demonstrates how you are able to bind to different data source with editing enabled, and the grid will pick up editors based on the column data types.

data.name: WebDataGrid_AllBehaviorsEnabled_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how the WebDataGrid works when all behaviors are Enabled. Behaviors (Activation, Selection, Paging, RowSelectors, Sorting, ColumnMoving, ColumnResizing, CellEditing, RowAdding, RowDeleting, ColumnFixing, Filtering)

data.name: WebDataGrid_ColumnCalculationFixing_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how to setup a frozen column look using the fixed column feature of the WebDataGrid. A column has been fixed to the right side of the WebDataGrid and a custom calculation is applied to the column.

data.name: WebDataGrid_CustomClientEvents_Description
data.value: This sample demonstrates how you can use the new click, keypress event on the WebDataGrid to grab the element and its information.

data.name: WebDataGridWithContextMenu_DeleteMessage
data.value: Are you sure, you want do delete {0} record?

data.name: WebDataGridWithContextMenu_RowInEditMode
data.value: Exit Edit mode for Row with ID ({0})

data.name: ColumnHiding_Desc
data.value: This sample shows how to hide specific columns in the WebDataGrid. The city and country columns have been hidden.

data.name: ColumnHiding_Desc_Client
data.value: This sample shows how to hide specific columns in the WebDataGrid using the CSOM. Check or Uncheck the checkboxes below to show/hide a column.

data.name: WebDataGrid_SwitchingDataSourcesontheFly_Description
data.value: Select a data source from the drop down to switch data sources.

data.name: WebDataGrid_SwitchingDataSourcesontheFly_Title
data.value: WebDataGrid - Switching Data Sources on the Fly

data.name: DelarativeDataBinding_AccessDataSourceDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to AccessDataSource control.

data.name: DelarativeDataBinding_DomainDataSourceDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to DomainDataSource control.

data.name: DelarativeDataBinding_LinqDataSourceDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to LinqDataSource control.

data.name: DelarativeDataBinding_SqlDataSourceDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to SqlDataSource control.

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_DataTableDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to a DataTable.

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_DataTableTitle
data.value: Simple Data Binding to a DataTable

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_IBindingListDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to a IBindingList.

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_IBindingListTitle
data.value: Simple Data Binding to a BindingList

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_IEnumerableDescription
data.value: The WebDataGrid is a powerful control that supports a variety of different data sources. This sample shows how to bind the grid to a IEnumerable Collection. The grid's BoundDataFields are created from code-behind.

data.name: ProgramaticDataBinding_IEnumerableTitle
data.value: Simple Data Binding to an IEnumerable

data.name: WebDataGridExcelExporter_Description
data.value: Exporting data to Microsoft Excel has never been easier! The power of WebDataGrid is extended by adding this great feature. You can either export only visible (grid loaded) data, or choose to export all data in data source. Everything with a single click and many method overloads. Check out the source code for details

data.name: WebDataGridExcelExporter_Title
data.value: Exporting data to Excel(tm) using Excel Exporter for WebDataGrid
